Settings → Transcription was the authoritative store, and `RecordingSession.start()` re-read it on every Record press — so a toolbar pick was silently clobbered the moment you started the next recording. Net effect a tester caught: "наоборот не работает и в итоге язык настроек возвращает язык в тулбаре". Now the toolbar pick propagates to the mode-appropriate Settings slot (`defaultTranscriptionLocale` for meeting, `voiceNoteLocale` for voice notes, `dictationLocale` for dictation) so it survives Record AND shows up next time you open Settings. The Settings → toolbar mirror was already in place (1.0.7.2) — now extended to listen on all three locale fields plus `currentMode` so picking a per-mode override in Settings while in that mode pulls through immediately.
  • **Click-to-copy on the version pill.** The `v1.0.7.3` line in the sidebar footer and the `Version 1.0.7.3 (30)` line in the About header are both tappable — clicking copies `Daisy 1.0.7.3 (30)` to clipboard with a toast confirmation. Build number comes from `CFBundleVersion` (the same value Sparkle uses to compare appcast entries), so a paste into a support thread pinpoints the exact bundle the user is running rather than the marketing version alone. No visual change to either pill — `.help()` tooltip on hover is the only affordance signal, per the "no icons" constraint.

item.title: Daisy 1.0.7.4
item.description:
What's new in 1.0.7.4

  • **Lighter live transcription — new "Lite" mode, now the default (build 46).** Settings → Transcription → Live transcription is now a three-way choice: Full / Lite / Off. Lite trims the live decode (fewer Neural Engine workers, greedy sampling, no temperature fallbacks) and runs every ~3.5s instead of every 2s — much lighter on long meetings and on battery, with a slightly rougher live preview. On macOS 26 Lite uses Apple's on-device SpeechAnalyzer, whose model lives in the system asset catalog, so it adds nothing to Daisy's memory; on macOS 14–15, or for languages/locales it doesn't support, Lite transparently falls back to a light Whisper profile. The transcript written on Stop is always full-quality Whisper (with diarization) regardless of this setting, and dictation always uses live. Existing users who had live transcription on are migrated to Lite; those who had it off stay off.
  • **Close button on the recording window (build 46).** The menu-bar popover now has an ✕ to dismiss it. Recording keeps running in the background — reopen the window any time from the menu-bar icon.
  • **Consent reminder moved to the bottom of the recording window (build 46).** The "Always get consent when transcribing others" strip now sits at the bottom instead of pushing the transcript down from the top.
  • **Recording no longer goes silent when you connect Bluetooth mid-meeting (build 46).** Previously, connecting AirPods (or any Bluetooth output) while recording on the built-in mic could let macOS quietly switch the input to the Bluetooth microphone, which often captured silence — the timer kept running but your voice wasn't recorded. Daisy now keeps recording on the microphone it started with when a Bluetooth device appears for output, and surfaces a clear warning if an input ever stops delivering audio instead of silently recording nothing. To record through a Bluetooth mic on purpose, pick it in Settings.

item.title: Daisy 1.0.7.5
item.description:
What's new in 1.0.7.5

  • **Recordings could be discarded on Stop after a mic/route change — fixed (build 47).** When the input device changed during a session (Bluetooth connecting, or a device re-initialisation right after Start), Daisy rolls the audio into a new file part. The Stop-time "did we capture anything?" check only looked at the original file, so it could wrongly decide the session was empty and delete the whole thing — losing the recording **and** its transcript even though audio and a live transcript existed. Daisy now counts captured audio across all file parts, and never discards a session that produced a transcript (this also covers "Don't record audio" mode, where the transcript is the whole point). If you saw a recording vanish on Stop in 1.0.7.4, this is the fix.
  • **Recording widget no longer flickers during "Preparing" (build 47).** The flower's centre stayed a calm, static white while the petals do the loading shimmer — the brief amber blink in the middle during start-up is gone.

item.title: Daisy 1.0.7.6
item.description:
What's new in 1.0.7.6

  • **A recording is never silently discarded on Stop (build 48).** If a session ends with no captured audio and no transcript — for example when live transcription produced nothing — Daisy now keeps the recording and tells you, instead of quietly removing it. A genuinely empty, very short accidental tap still cleans itself up, but with a notice rather than in silence.
  • **Calendar picker now matches the menu bar (build 48).** The menu-bar "next meeting" label and the recording popover's calendar picker now use the same look-ahead window, so an event the menu bar shows is always selectable in the picker. Previously an event roughly 6–8 hours out appeared in the menu bar but the picker reported "No upcoming events."
  • **Bigger recording widget (build 48).** The floating daisy widget and its panel are about 10% larger.

item.title: Daisy 1.0.7.7
item.description:
What's new in 1.0.7.7

  • The recording flower is calmer and clearer: the loader no longer stair-steps on ProMotion displays, the centre stays steady (no flicker) while a summary is generating, and the "done" pop is gentler.
  • Daisy now respects Reduce Motion (System Settings → Accessibility → Display): the spinning loader and the celebration bounce are replaced with a calm, static state.
  • The three recording modes — meeting, dictation, voice note — now read at matched brightness, so no mode looks louder or dimmer than the others.
  • Recording sounds reworked: a clear chime now plays the moment your transcript is ready (in sync with the flower), and a distinct, gentle cue plays if a recording fails. The pause / resume / stop clicks were removed — they fired mid-recording and could leak into meetings played through speakers.
  • Better acoustic-echo cleanup: when you record a meeting through your speakers instead of headphones, far fewer duplicate lines slip into the transcript. Daisy now also catches echoes when one side captured a whole sentence and the other split it into pieces.

item.title: Daisy 1.0.7.8
item.description:
What's new in 1.0.7.8

  • System audio — the remote side of your meetings — is now reliably saved. Fixed a long-standing bug where the system-audio recording could end up empty (0 bytes) even though the meeting was captured.
  • Daisy now warns you mid-meeting if system audio is coming through silent (DRM-protected playback, or a macOS capture glitch), so you find out before the meeting ends rather than after.
  • Auto-stop no longer cuts you off mid-sentence. After a calendar meeting's scheduled end, Daisy waits for a stretch of silence before it stops & saves (the 30-second "Keep going" warning still applies).
  • The microphone now auto-recovers if it opens on a dead audio device at the very start of a recording, instead of silently capturing nothing.
  • Better acoustic-echo cleanup when you record meetings through speakers instead of headphones — far fewer duplicate lines slip into the transcript.
  • Starting a new recording right after stopping no longer surfaces a harmless "final pass cancelled" event as an error.
  • Low disk space is now handled gracefully: when your drive is running low, Daisy automatically records the transcript only (skipping the heavy audio file) and tells you — so a long meeting can't fill your disk. Audio recorded before the switch is kept.
  • The recording flower is calmer and clearer: the loader is smooth on ProMotion displays, the centre no longer flickers, the "done" moment is gentler, and it now respects Reduce Motion (System Settings → Accessibility → Display).
  • Recording sounds reworked: a clear chime the moment your transcript is ready (in sync with the flower) and a distinct, gentle cue if a recording fails. The pause / resume / stop clicks were removed — they could leak into meetings played through speakers.
  • The three recording modes (meeting, dictation, voice note) now read at matched brightness, and dictation's colour is a clearer, brighter lilac.

item.title: Daisy 1.0.7.10
item.description:
What's new in 1.0.7.10

  • Recording reliably picks up your voice. Daisy captures the microphone at a lower level (Core Audio), fixing cases where a recording came back empty or missed your side — most often the built-in mic or a wired headset on macOS 26, where the audio engine could lock onto the wrong sample rate and capture nothing.
  • The recording flower is alive again. Its petals move with your voice while you record — under heavy live transcription they could sit frozen even though audio and the transcript were flowing; now they track the live spectrum.
  • Daisy can recognize people by their calendar email, not just their voice. When a recording is tied to a calendar meeting, Daisy matches an attendee to a speaker you've already named — by their invite address — so they're still recognized on a day their voice sounds different (a new mic, a cold, speakerphone).
  • A new "Match known speakers" control in Settings → Speakers, with three modes. **Automatic** (the default, and how Daisy has always worked) labels a recognized person as soon as a recording finishes. **Suggest** recognizes them but waits — the name appears as a suggestion in the recording's "Name the speakers" card, and you confirm before it's applied. **Off** turns cross-meeting auto-labeling off entirely; speakers stay "Remote A / B" until you name them (fingerprints are still saved, so you can switch it back on anytime).
  • A speaker editor. Open any known speaker in Settings to add their email addresses (so calendar invites match them) and notes — role, company, how you met — or forget them. It also lists every recording where you've named them, so you can jump straight to a past meeting.
  • Smoother capture under load. The microphone's disk writing and audio analysis run off the real-time audio thread, so heavy live transcription competes less with capture — fewer glitches on busy machines.
  • Each person in the Known speakers list now opens a detail card when clicked.
  • The "only your voice" notice (shown when Screen Recording is off) is now a single tap that opens the permission settings.
  • Your existing speakers keep working unchanged: the new fields start empty, the new setting defaults to Automatic, and voice fingerprints, emails, and notes all stay on your Mac.

item.title: Daisy 1.0.7.11
item.description:
What's new in 1.0.7.11

  • Switching your audio device while recording — plugging in wired headphones, connecting Bluetooth, or moving to speakers — no longer breaks transcription. Before, if the new device ran at a different sample rate (common with wired headsets), the live transcript would freeze and that stretch went missing from the saved transcript too — even though the flower showed Daisy was still hearing you. Daisy now adapts to the new device on the fly and keeps transcribing without a gap.

item.title: Daisy 1.0.7.12
item.description:
What's new in 1.0.7.12

  • A long recording could lose its audio if something refreshed your library mid-session. A recording longer than five minutes has audio on disk but no transcript yet (the transcript is written when you press Stop), and Daisy's "tidy up leftover empty folders" cleanup could mistake the in-progress recording for leftover junk and remove it — most often when another app queried Daisy through its local server (for example an AI assistant connected to Daisy), or when the History list refreshed while you were still recording. Daisy now always protects the recording in progress, so it is never cleaned up until it has been safely saved.

item.title: Daisy 1.0.7.13
item.description:
What's new in 1.0.7.13

  • The floating widget is a bit smaller now, so it sits more quietly out of your way while you work.
  • If you quit Daisy while it's still recording, it now asks you first — and if you confirm, it finalizes and saves the recording (audio and the transcript so far) before closing, instead of losing it.
  • "Draft follow-up" now always writes a follow-up when you ask for it, even for a conversation Daisy would otherwise treat as internal.
  • Recording from your built-in mic no longer goes silent when you're playing audio through Bluetooth headphones — Daisy now avoids the headset's low-quality Bluetooth mic from the start of a recording (it already switched away from it mid-recording).
  • Long transcript lines no longer spill past the edge of their card.

item.title: Daisy 1.0.7.14
item.description:
What's new in 1.0.7.14

  • You can now pick which on-device model powers **dictation** in **Settings → Transcription → Dictation engine**: **Whisper** (default) or the new, experimental **Parakeet** — a faster streaming model that covers 25 European languages including Russian. Switching to Parakeet downloads its model once (~600 MB) with a visible progress bar. Meetings always use Whisper, and if Parakeet can't run, dictation falls back to Whisper automatically.

item.title: Daisy 1.0.7.15
item.description:
What's new in 1.0.7.15

  • **You'll know when the other side isn't being recorded.** If Daisy can't capture a meeting's other side (Screen Recording permission missing, or audio playing to a device that can't be captured), the floating flower's center turns red and a banner appears in the window and the menu-bar panel — instead of quietly recording only your voice.
  • **Transcription settings, simplified.** Model, dictation engine, language, and live-transcript detail now live in one block with plain names (Default / Highest Accuracy, Default / Faster) and a visible model-download progress.
  • **Progress while summaries and follow-ups generate.** Re-summarizing or drafting a follow-up now shows an inline spinner, not just a quick toast.
  • Library transcript no longer spills past the edge of its card, and speaker labels render in **bold** instead of showing raw `**` asterisks.
  • The floating widget now matches Daisy's logo and gently "breathes" while it's working.

item.title: Daisy 1.0.7.16
item.description:
What's new in 1.0.7.16

  • **Simpler auto-start settings.** The Always / Selective / Prompt / Manual buttons are now a single "Auto-record" dropdown with plain-language choices — All meetings, Meetings with a link, Ask me first, Off — living in the Meetings settings group. The fiddly "Detection delay" knob is gone; Daisy just starts when a meeting app opens, and if it ever jumps the gun you can stop it from the "Recording started" notification.
  • **Tidier Settings, regrouped.** Plain-language labels throughout ("Capture system audio" → "Record the other side", "Play sound…" → "Notification sounds", the floating recorder is just "Floating widget"), long sub-labels trimmed, and rows moved to where they belong — "Record the other side" sits with the Meetings options, "Notification sounds" with the other notifications, and the floating widget with Audio & devices. Shortcut rows read simply too ("Dictation," "Hold to talk, release to paste").
  • **Auto-stop in one choice.** The "Stop when the meeting wraps up" toggle and the separate "Grace period" picker are now a single "Stop when the meeting ends" dropdown — Off, At the scheduled end, or 5 minutes after.
  • **Periodic screenshots paused.** The "Capture screenshots periodically" option has been removed for now while we rework it.
  • **Speaker settings, combined and in plain words.** "Diarization" and "Speaker matching" are now one "Speakers" block, and the jargon is gone — "Diarize microphone too" → "Split voices in my mic", "Suppress acoustic echo" → "Remove echo from my mic", "Match known speakers" → "Name known people".
  • **Settings, reorganized for clarity.** A new **Recording** tab gathers your microphone, shortcuts, meeting auto-record, and on-screen widget in one place, so **General** is now just Profile, Storage, Privacy, and Notifications. Storage split into Storage / Privacy ("Delete audio after"); the "Recordings folder" is named for what it holds; model and engine choices read in plain words (Standard / Most accurate / Faster); and the model-cache row sits at the end of Transcription.
  • **Notion lives with your other connections.** Sending finished recordings to Notion now sits on the **Connections** page next to your MCP integrations, instead of buried under Settings → Storage.
  • **One word for a recording.** Daisy now consistently calls a saved meeting a "recording" (not "session"), and the auto-record prompt asks about a "meeting" rather than a "call."
  • **Calendar auto-record now starts right on schedule.** Previously a meeting could begin recording up to ~30 seconds early and at an unpredictable moment (the calendar was only checked every 15 seconds). Daisy now arms a precise timer and starts recording exactly 5 seconds before the meeting's scheduled start — enough to catch the opening words, without the random pre-roll. If Daisy launches or wakes after a meeting has already begun, it still starts immediately (within the first 2 minutes).
  • **The "couldn't reach the other side" warning now clears itself.** If the other side was simply quiet for the first couple of minutes, Daisy could show the "Only your voice" banner — and then keep showing it even after the other side started talking. The warning now disappears the moment real audio is captured from the other side (and the floating flower's center returns to normal).
  • **Transcript view renders markdown properly.** Headings, the recording subtitle, and bullet/checklist lines now display as formatted text instead of showing raw `#`, `>`, and `-` characters (speaker labels were already bold).

item.title: Daisy 1.0.7.17
item.description:
What's new in 1.0.7.17

  • Long recordings no longer lose parts of the transcript. When you press Stop, Daisy now re-transcribes the complete recording from the saved audio on disk — so the final transcript and speaker labels cover the whole session, even long or fast-paced meetings where the live preview fell behind.
  • The offline pass on Stop now spans the entire recording (not just the most recent stretch), so summaries and "who said what" are built from the full conversation.

item.title: Daisy 1.0.7.18
item.description:
What's new in 1.0.7.18

  • Daisy now uses a single dark theme throughout — matching the brand. The app no longer follows your system Light/Dark appearance; it always renders dark.

item.title: Daisy 1.0.7.19
item.description:
What's new in 1.0.7.19

  • Text selection in the summary finally works like text: drag across the lede, sections, bullets and action items in one go, ⌘A selects the whole summary, ⌘C copies clean text. Previously selection stopped at every line.
  • Auto-stop got a brain transplant. It now reliably notices when a meeting is over: the "is anyone still talking" check no longer gets stuck on a frozen system-audio level or tripped by keyboard clicks and fan noise (both could keep a finished meeting recording for an extra half-hour). It also stops after ~10 minutes of total silence even before the scheduled end — the "everyone left early" case.
  • New: "Ask before auto-stopping" (Settings → Recording). Instead of stopping on its own, Daisy sends a notification — Stop & save, 10 more minutes, or 30 more minutes.
  • Meetings auto-started from your calendar now take the event's title instead of a "Meeting 2026-06-12 12:31" placeholder.
  • Dictation is snappier on release: the model is pre-warmed at launch, the final decode uses a lighter profile tuned for dictation, and the throwaway live pass no longer delays the paste.
  • New: "Live preview while dictating" (Settings → General → Appearance) — an experimental streaming engine shows words ~0.6 s behind your speech while you hold the key (the pasted text is still produced by the main engine).
  • Model downloads are finally visible: the main window and the menu-bar popover now show a progress banner — checking, downloading with a percent, loading — instead of hiding it in a tooltip.
  • The Record button is green when idle. Orange now always means "the mic is (about to be) live" — pause/resume, the widget, the warnings — so a parked app no longer looks like it's recording.
  • Recording shortcuts: assigning a key combo while two recorder fields were active could write the combo to the wrong action (e.g. Dictation's combo landing on Voice note). Only one recorder listens at a time now.
  • The elapsed timer moved into the record button in the popover; the redundant status caption and pulsing dot are gone.
  • ⌘R (View → Refresh Meetings) re-reads your calendar on demand — no more waiting for the next automatic tick after adding an event.
  • Library search is much faster on large libraries (token-index prefilter; same results).
  • Help → Send Log Report: collects the last 24 h of Daisy's logs plus an environment summary and opens a pre-addressed email — you review it and press Send. For when something misbehaves and you want us to actually see why.
  • Settings → Summary now tells you exactly why a provider is unavailable instead of a bare "Unavailable". For Apple Intelligence it names the actual cause — not turned on, still downloading, this Mac isn't eligible, or (a common one) your Mac and Siri are set to different languages — with what to do about it.
  • Privacy, enforced in code: the on-device audio engines (speaker diarization, voice detection, the Parakeet dictation engine) are now hard-blocked from the network except during explicit model downloads. A damaged model cache can no longer trigger a silent re-download mid-meeting — it fails visibly instead.
  • The Faster dictation engine (Parakeet) picks up ~8% quicker decoding from the updated on-device audio stack.
  • New in About: "Get beta updates" — opt in to receive the newest builds first. Off by default; everyone else keeps getting stable releases only.
  • Copying got smart about where you paste. Every Copy button now puts two flavors on the clipboard at once: paste into Slack, Notion, Gmail or Apple Notes and it lands formatted — real headings, nested bullets, checkboxes; paste into Obsidian, Claude or any plain-text editor and you get clean markdown. Copy summary now produces proper markdown instead of an ALL-CAPS text dump, and the session ⋯ menu gains "Copy as Markdown" (raw markdown only) and "Copy for Obsidian" (with the YAML frontmatter block for vault filing).
  • Ollama summaries are more honest and less fiddly. The model menu now lists the models you've actually pulled (read live from Ollama) instead of a fixed catalog that drifted out of date. And if you pick one of Ollama's `:cloud` models, Settings now says plainly that your local Ollama proxies the request to ollama.com — so the transcript leaves your Mac — instead of still labelling it "local".
  • Permissions are asked only when you need them. First launch now asks for just the two things dictation needs — Microphone and Accessibility. Screen Recording (to capture the other side of a meeting) is requested the first time you actually record a meeting, and Calendar only when you connect one — so the wall of system prompts at first run is gone. Settings → Permissions is grouped to match ("Enough for dictation" vs "For meeting recording"), with a "Don't remind me" toggle to mute the in-app nudge.
  • Dictation has its own home: a "Dictation" page in the sidebar with a Vocabulary you can teach (custom word replacements that fix names, brands and jargon the model mishears, applied right before the text is pasted) and a rolling 24-hour history of what you dictated — tap any entry to copy it again, auto-cleared after a day.
  • New: Menu-bar-only (compact) mode (Settings → General → Appearance). Daisy hides the main window and lives in the menu bar + floating widget, like a focused dictation tool; open the window any time from the menu-bar ⋯ menu, or flip it off for the full app.
  • New: delete recordings in bulk (Settings → General → Storage) — all of them or just one folder, with a confirmation and a live count. The audio-only row is now "Delete audio files" (keeps your transcripts), so the two delete actions read as clearly different.
  • Recordings that picked up no speech no longer get a made-up summary — instead of the model inventing an apologetic "the recording failed" write-up, Daisy shows a clean "nothing to summarize" state.
  • Home no longer nags you to connect a calendar: the "Today" block only appears once a calendar is actually connected.

item.title: Daisy 1.0.7.20
item.description:
What's new in 1.0.7.20

  • Dictation vocabulary now does two jobs. On the Dictation page → Vocabulary → Add word, leave the toggle off to **teach Daisy a word** — a name, brand, or piece of jargon — so it spells and capitalises it the way you want; flip "Correct a misspelling" on to make a **replacement rule** (misheard → fix), the way the dictionary worked before. Each entry shows which kind it is, and you can edit or delete it inline.
  • Words you teach don't just get fixed after the fact — they also nudge the transcription engine to actually hear them, so a name it used to mangle comes out right more often to begin with.
  • Calmer colours. The bright amber accent that tinted selected filters, links and controls is now a light brown, and the Record button is a warm beige instead of green — less visual noise across the warm interface. The brown row-selection highlight is unchanged.

item.title: Daisy 1.0.7.21
item.description:
What's new in 1.0.7.21

  • **Import from Apple Voice Memos.** Turn on Settings → Recording → "Import Voice Memos" and Daisy checks once a day, transcribes any new recordings on your Mac, and drops a plain-text note into a "Voice Memos" folder alongside your transcripts. No summaries, no cloud — the audio never leaves your Mac. iCloud recordings synced from your iPhone get picked up too.
  • One-time setup: Daisy needs Full Disk Access to read your Voice Memos library, and points you straight to the right System Settings pane. Already have a back catalogue? Hit "Process existing recordings" to transcribe everything; otherwise Daisy only imports new memos from when you switch it on.

item.title: Daisy 1.0.7.22
item.description:
What's new in 1.0.7.22

  • **Long transcripts no longer get cut off.** A long meeting (40+ minutes) could render only partway in the Library with no way to scroll to the rest. The transcript now lives in its own scrollable pane, so you can read — and select — all of it, however long the recording.
  • **Compact menu bar, reworked.** With "Compact menu bar" on (Settings → General → Appearance), clicking the menu-bar icon now opens a quick menu — Summarize, Open Library, Settings, New recording, Quit — instead of the full transcription window. It no longer hides your Dock icon or the app menus.
  • **Calmer Record button, with the shortcut on it.** The Record button in the menu-bar popover is now the same warm beige as the main window (no more green), and the main window's Record button shows your record shortcut right on it.
  • **Dictation page tidy.** "Add word" and "Clear history" moved to the top-right of their sections as quick capsule buttons.
  • **Voice Memos import: pick where it saves.** If you import Apple Voice Memos, you can now choose the exact folder the transcripts land in — separate from your meeting sessions — so they drop straight into your notes/vault.
  • **Rename a recording** — click its title at the top of the Library view and type a new one; it saves when you press Return or click away.
  • **Summary, Follow-up and Transcript are now three separate panels**, each with its own copy button — the follow-up draft got pulled out into its own section. The Summarize button now shows a spinner while it's working.
  • Fixes: the Record button no longer briefly flashes a red "Try again" right after a cold launch; the top of the window no longer shows a white strip in light system mode; and a meeting that's too long for Apple Intelligence's on-device model now tells you to switch to a cloud provider instead of failing silently.
  • Smaller polish: the status line no longer repeats what the Record button already says, the Library toolbar drops a redundant Copy button (every copy option still lives in the ⋯ menu), and the automatic-update control is now a switch.

item.title: Daisy 1.0.7.23
item.description:
What's new in 1.0.7.23

  • **Copy any one part of a session.** The ⋯ menu in the Library now has **Copy Summary**, **Copy Follow-up** and **Copy Transcript** — each copies just that section as Markdown — plus **Copy all** for the whole thing. (The old "Copy as Markdown / Copy for Obsidian" pair is gone: the only difference between them was a metadata header, and Daisy's routing already handles vault filing.)
  • **The dictation flower reacts to your voice again.** While dictating, the petals barely moved as you spoke; they now respond with the same sensitivity as meeting recording.
  • Smaller polish: the ⋯ button in the Library toolbar is now a proper rounded capsule instead of a tight square.

item.title: Daisy 1.0.7.24
item.description:
What's new in 1.0.7.24

  • **Custom Library folders.** Settings → General → Folders now lets you create, rename and delete your own folders. Private, Work and Calls became editable too (Inbox and Notes stay fixed). New folders show up in the sidebar and the "Move to folder…" menu; deleting a folder moves its recordings to Inbox.
  • **Pick a default folder for meetings.** Choose where calendar-bound and auto-started meetings get filed — it was always "Work". The folder you pick as the default can't be deleted until you switch the default away from it.
  • **The Transcript panel shows just the conversation.** It no longer repeats the recording's title and "recorded …" line at the top (that's already in the header above), and "Copy Transcript" copies the clean transcript.
  • **Tag from the toolbar.** A recording's tag now lives in a capsule next to Summarize — click it to type a new tag or pick one you've used before.

item.title: Daisy 1.0.7.25
item.description:
What's new in 1.0.7.25

  • The floating widget's "Hide for…" menu now has a real **Hide for 24 hours** — the old "Hide for the day" actually brought the widget back after 8 hours. Hiding it now also sticks if you quit and reopen Daisy, instead of reappearing on the next launch.
  • The **Dictation** page now uses the same native tabs as Settings, and **Add word** is available from both the Vocabulary and History tabs (it used to disappear on History).
  • **Voice Memos import** moved to Settings → Transcription and got a tidier layout: the save folder and its "Choose folder…" picker now share one row, and **Process existing recordings** shows live progress and tells you how many it imported when it's done.
  • The Full Disk Access request for Voice Memos now lives in Settings → Permissions, clearly marked as needed only for Voice Memos.

item.title: Daisy 1.0.7.26
item.description:
What's new in 1.0.7.26

  • Daisy now recovers recordings interrupted by a crash, power loss, or force-quit. If your Mac dies mid-meeting, the recording is no longer lost — on the next launch Daisy rebuilds the transcript from the audio it had already saved and adds it to your Library.
  • Recordings pause cleanly when your Mac goes to sleep and resume when it wakes; a graceful restart or shutdown saves the recording in progress.
  • If you've set Storage to "Don't record audio", Daisy now warns that an interrupted recording can't be recovered in that mode — only sessions you finish keep their transcript.

item.title: Daisy 1.0.7.27
item.description:
What's new in 1.0.7.27

  • Automatic update checks now run on a defined hourly schedule. Previously the check cadence was unspecified, so some installs could go a long time without noticing a new build was available.
  • The Follow-up section now scrolls all the way through long drafts. It was being clipped at its frame, so the bottom of a longer follow-up couldn't be reached.
  • The widget's "Hide for 24 hours" is now "Hide for today" — it hides until the next midnight and comes back the following day, instead of a rolling 24-hour window.
  • The Home view content is now width-capped and centered to match the Settings pages, instead of stretching edge to edge.
  • Tidied the Permissions screen: simpler "Don't remind me" wording, fewer redundant section footers, and the Full Disk Access badge now reads "Optional".
  • The dictation history "Clear" button is now styled neutrally rather than as a destructive red action.
  • "Send Log Report…" now includes more diagnostics — update-checker state, per-session audio-capture outcomes, and auto-stop decisions — so problems can be pinned down from a single report.

item.title: Daisy 1.0.7.28
item.description:
What's new in 1.0.7.28

  • Long Summary and Follow-up sections no longer get cut off at the bottom of their card. They now size to their content and scroll inside the card when long, so nothing is hidden out of reach.

item.title: Daisy 1.0.7.29
item.description:
What's new in 1.0.7.29

  • Fixed a crash that could close Daisy during window layout (introduced in 1.0.7.28 while making Summary and Follow-up scroll). Those sections still size to their content and scroll when long — without the crash.

item.title: Daisy 1.0.7.30
item.description:
What's new in 1.0.7.30

  • Updated the on-device speech engine (FluidAudio 0.15.4). Parakeet transcription now uses the v3 model with broader language coverage (25 languages), and speaker diarization moves to the newer pyannote Community-1 model for cleaner speaker separation. Recording and transcription work exactly as before — the underlying models are just newer and more capable. The updated models download automatically the first time they're needed.
